files.name: Recommendation-Letter-Professor.docx
files.description: Academic recommendation letter from a college professor.
files.content:
LETTER OF RECOMMENDATION

To Whom It May Concern,

I am pleased to recommend Mr. Mahmud Hasan, whom I have taught for two years at
Dhaka College in the Higher Secondary science programme. Mahmud consistently
ranked among the top students of his cohort and demonstrated exceptional aptitude
in mathematics, physics, and computer studies.

Beyond academics, he showed strong leadership as the secretary of the college
science club and organised the inter-college programming olympiad in 2024. He is
disciplined, curious, and dependable.

I recommend him without reservation for admission to your undergraduate programme.

Sincerely,
Dr. Anwar Kabir
Associate Professor, Department of Physics
Dhaka College    Date: 24 February 2026

From: Mahmud Hasan, 1st Year, Computer Science and Engineering

Subject: Request for allotment of a residential seat for the 2026 session.

Respected Sir,
I am a newly admitted student from Rajshahi with no local guardian in Dhaka.
I respectfully request the allotment of a seat in your hall so that I can attend
classes regularly. I have attached my admission slip, medical clearance, and
guardian consent. I will abide by all hall rules.

Yours faithfully,
Mahmud Hasan    Date: 04 March 2026

PAPERHUB BANGLADESH — MONTHLY DOCUMENT REVIEW REPORT (MAY 2026)
Prepared by: Rajdip Roy, Document Review Officer

1. Volume
   Documents received .................... 142
   Documents reviewed and cleared ........ 104
   Returned for correction ...............  22
   Pending at month end ..................  16

2. Turnaround
   Average review time ................... 1.8 business days
   High-priority average ................. 0.9 business days

3. Common Issues
   - Mismatched NID and birth-registration dates (9 cases)
   - Bank solvency letters missing official seal (6 cases)
   - Illegible scan quality on transcripts (4 cases)

4. Recommendation
   Introduce an automated pre-check for NID/birth-date consistency before
   documents enter the manual review queue.

Signed: Rajdip Roy    Date: 31 May 2026

files.name: User-Access-Control-Policy.pdf
files.description: Role-based access control policy for the platform.
files.summary: Access policy v3 approved and enforced.
files.content:
USER ACCESS CONTROL POLICY (v3)

1. Roles
   - User: upload documents, track submissions, access own payments.
   - Officer: review/verify documents, manage the review queue.
   - Admin: full platform governance, analytics, and policy.

2. Principle of Least Privilege
   Each account receives only the permissions required for its role.

3. Authentication
   Two-factor authentication is mandatory for officer and admin accounts.

4. Audit
   All privileged actions are logged and reviewed monthly.

Owner: Rajesh Biswas, Platform Administrator    Effective: 01 June 2026

files.content:
USER ACCESS CONTROL POLICY (v3)

1. Roles
   - User: upload documents, track submissions, access own payments.
   - Officer: review/verify documents, manage the review queue.
   - Admin: full platform governance, analytics, and policy.

   Each account receives only the permissions required for its role.

3. Authentication
   Two-factor authentication is mandatory for officer and admin accounts.

4. Audit
   All privileged actions are logged and reviewed monthly.

Owner: Rajesh Biswas, Platform Administrator    Effective: 01 June 2026

USER ACCESS CONTROL POLICY (v3)

1. Roles
   - User: upload documents, track submissions, access own payments.
   - Officer: review/verify documents, manage the review queue.
   - Admin: full platform governance, analytics, and policy.

2. Principle of Least Privilege
   Each account receives only the permissions required for its role.

3. Authentication
   Two-factor authentication is mandatory for officer and admin accounts.

4. Audit
   All privileged actions are logged and reviewed monthly.
